title: AN ACT DECLARING MARCH TEN OF EVERY YEAR AS A SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY IN THE PROVINCE OF ABRA TO BE KNOWN AS ABRA DAY

chunks.text:
H. NO. 23951 / 88 OG No. 19, 2721 (May 11, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7281, March 24, 1992 ]

AN ACT DECLARING MARCH TEN OF EVERY YEAR AS A SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY IN THE PROVINCE OF ABRA TO BE KNOWN AS ABRA DAY

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. March Ten of every year is hereby declared as a special nonworking holiday in the Province of Abra to commemorate the creation of the Province, to be known as Abra Day.

SEC. 2. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.) RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on September 4, 1991 and January 23, 1992, respectively.

(Sgd.) ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.) CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: March 24, 1992

(Sgd.) CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
